objective: Repair or reject the German speech measurement instrument by separating VAD/boundary instability from matched-segment ASR content instability on the existing ChildLens development participants only.

scope.development_manifest_source: v1 frozen participant split, development rows only

ordered_diagnosis: D1 speech/non-speech segmentation and utterance-boundary stability
D2 transcript and language stability conditional on identical frozen audio segments
D3 boundary-mismatch attribution by comparing v1 independent-boundary agreement with v2 matched-segment agreement

text_embedding_diagnostic.pooling: attention-mask-aware mean pooling followed by L2 normalization
text_embedding_diagnostic.role: model-model semantic agreement diagnostic, not truth

public_canary.input: macOS German TTS sentence with no ChildLens ancestry

shared_segmentation.audio: 16 kHz mono signed 16-bit PCM extracted from each frozen v1 development sample span
shared_segmentation.long_segment_split: deterministic contiguous chunks no longer than 12 seconds; discard terminal remainder shorter than 300 ms

released_annotation_support.purpose: acceptance support and signal-coverage comparison only

shared_segmentation.asr_input: both ASR systems receive byte-identical concatenations of the same accepted frozen VAD segments with 500 ms silence separators

vad_self_consistency.operation: prepend and append 250 ms digital silence, rerun identical VAD, subtract the leading offset

transcript_timing_robustness.operation: expand every accepted source segment by 250 ms on each available side without overlap or wrap

timing: VAD speech seconds and sample-span coverage
matched_transcript: item-level automatic language identification and model-model agreement
primary and sensitivity nonempty item fractions
normalized character similarity on identical segments
multilingual sentence-embedding cosine on identical segments
primary and sensitivity transcript self-consistency under 250 ms boundary expansion
attribution: v2 matched-segment character similarity minus v1 independent-boundary character similarity
metrics.uncertainty: participant-cluster percentile bootstrap, 10000 replicates, 90% interval, fixed seed 20260706

G1_signal_and_vad.reason: Remediation-specific timing gates are stricter than v1 boundary gates because one shared segmenter must be mechanically stable before ASR content can be isolated.

G2_matched_asr.reason: The v1 coverage, language, character-median, and character-lower thresholds are unchanged. Embedding gates are additive and allow orthographic variation without lowering lexical stability requirements.

G3_timing_shift_robustness.reason: A 250 ms boundary expansion should not materially change a stable transcript instrument.

attribution_rules.boundary_mismatch_major_contributor: v2 matched character median improves by at least 0.20 over v1 and v2 matched character gate passes
attribution_rules.inadequate_german_asr: G1 passes but G2 or G3 fails
attribution_rules.inadequate_vad_boundary_estimation: signal-yield subgates pass but VAD self-consistency subgates fail
attribution_rules.insufficient_or_unsuitable_childlens_spe: segment yield, speech seconds, or annotation-support coverage fails
attribution_rules.human_validation_limit: model-model gates can establish stability but never transcription accuracy or ground truth

stop_rules: Stop if any required instrument hash or v1 binding fails.
A G1 failure prevents any pass or locked recommendation. The already bounded matched-segment ASR diagnostic may still complete to distinguish VAD/signal failure from ASR-content failure.
Stop after matched transcript diagnosis if G2 or G3 fails.
Do not introduce another ASR, VAD, aligner, text model, threshold, or segmentation rule post hoc.
A pass recommends only a separately authorized locked alignment evaluation; it does not authorize it.
Never generate simulator episodes or train side-cue conditions in this remediation.
